title: First Ask If There Is Distance
source_basis: Mode: Critique. The active claim was pressured by Dogen-style practice-realization, Shinran Other Power, Dzogchen direct recognition, and Huangbo search-refusal cases.

Thinking method source: Dogen's practice-realization, reused as a reasoning lens by dropping the assumption that practice is a bridge to a later state. Critique of the method: this lens can hide real sequence, effort, screening, and safety needs, so it was checked against Dzogchen stabilization and Shinran's vow-held trust.

Primary-text comparison: Dogen's Bendowa passage on practice and realization as inseparable, with no boundary in realization and no beginning in practice, https://buddhismnow.com/2023/10/08/practice-within-realisation-dogen/

Primary-text comparison: Shinran's letter on Other Power, no calculation, and no working as true working, https://shinranworks.com/letters/a-collection-of-letters-zensho-text/what-we-call-other-power/

Primary-text comparison: Longchen Rabjam's Trekcho instruction names direct introduction but also sustaining the experience, vows, devotion, guru reliance, renunciation, and conduct, https://www.lotsawahouse.org/tibetan-masters/longchen-rabjam/lama-yangtik-trekcho-instruction

Primary-text pressure: Huangbo's Transmission of Mind warns that seeking externally loses what is sought, while still speaking inside a teacher-student transmission, https://groveatlantic.com/book/the-zen-teaching-of-huang-po/
Near-neighbor pressure: Steven Heine on Dogen's realization-based language and the critique of linear, instrumental readings, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/12/2/81

Near-neighbor pressure: Glenn Hartelius on modern nondual state language being incompatible with lineage-based Advaita and weak as psychology, https://digitalcommons.ciis.edu/ijts-transpersonalstudies/vol42/iss2/2/

Empirical-adjacent practice safety: Lindahl et al., Varieties of Contemplative Experience, on teacher, community, appraisal, worldview, and social support as risk or remedy factors, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239

Modern human-condition grounding: U.S. Surgeon General social connection advisory on loneliness, isolation, belonging, and support, local source card modern-human-condition-surgeon-general-social-connection-advisory, https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/surgeon-general-social-connection-advisory.pdf
original_claim: Do not ask where support moved until you know whether the teaching allowed a gap to exist. Some paths treat the distance between seeker and sought as real enough to cross. Other paths treat that imagined distance as the illness. In the second case, teacher, vow, posture, community, text, and conduct are not bridges to a later prize; they are guards against rebuilding the gap as private certainty, passivity, or isolation. The safer doctrine is modest: no-distance is not no-support.
why_it_might_be_new: The closest prior arguments already exist: Dogen practice-realization, Huangbo's search-refusal, Dzogchen direct recognition, Shinran Other Power, extended religious cognition, Asad's discursive tradition, Lindbeck's doctrine-as-grammar, VCE safety research, and recent internal records on continuity ecology and non-instrumental practice. The distinct move is to put seeker-sought distance before support-location coding. It says that continuity ecology applies cleanly only after a prior distance test, and that no-distance teachings have a special modern failure mode: unsupported seekers may mistake a denied metaphysical gap for permission to leave the ordinary supports that keep a life honest.
critique: The strongest counterargument is that no-distance traditions still use training, vows, teachers, ritual, ethics, and community in visible ways. Longchen Rabjam's Trekcho instruction, for example, joins direct recognition to sustained practice, guru reliance, vows, and conduct. Dogen's non-instrumental sitting still lives inside monastic rules and transmission. Shinran's no-calculation still depends on vow, lineage, and pastoral warning. If these traditions show the same support placement and failure clusters as instrumental paths, the distance test adds little and should be merged into continuity ecology. Huangbo is the harder anomaly: if search-refusal rejects both the bridge and any describable guard, even this narrowed model may still be too architectural.
next_research_directions: If this model is right, then no-distance texts should frame supports as guarding present realization, trust, or recognition rather than as bridges to a later result. If their supports are indistinguishable from ordinary instrumental supports, the model is weakened.

If this model is right, then modern solo users of no-distance teachings should report two failure clusters more often than guided practitioners: passivity disguised as freedom and isolation disguised as completion. If guided and unguided users show the same pattern, the cohort claim is weakened.

If this model is right, then adding a distance field to continuity-ecology coding should improve prediction of warning language and repair instructions. If tradition label, teacher quality, practice intensity, or ordinary social support predicts as well, the added field should be retired.
Close-read Huangbo, Dogen, Longchenpa, Shinran, and one gradual-path manual with the same question: does the source permit a real crossing, deny the crossing, or use both for different students?
Protocol improvement: before using any contemplative method as a lens, ask what distance the method quietly assumes between observer, practice, result, and life.

teaching_candidate.title: No Distance Is Not No Support
teaching_candidate.teaching_line: Do not use no-distance to leave what keeps you honest.
teaching_candidate.doctrine_claim: A teaching that denies a gap between seeker and sought does not remove support. It changes what support is for: not crossing a gap, but preventing false separation, private certainty, and lonely withdrawal.
teaching_candidate.body:
Some teachings say there is nowhere to go. This can free a person from chasing themselves. It can also become an excuse to leave the people, forms, and duties that keep life honest.

If a path says the sought is not elsewhere, ask what still holds you to the day: practice, promise, teacher, group, service, sleep, apology, work, or care for another person. If nothing is named, do not call that freedom. Call it unsupported.
teaching_candidate.target_human_problem: loneliness, withdrawal, meaning loss, and spiritualized avoidance among self-directed seekers
teaching_candidate.target_cohort: stable adults and older teens using direct-recognition, nondual, no-attainment, or practice-realization teachings outside ongoing guidance, especially through books, podcasts, videos, retreats, or online communities
teaching_candidate.pressure_survived: Survived close pressure from Dogen's practice-realization, Shinran's no-calculation, Dzogchen's direct recognition plus sustained support, Huangbo's search-refusal, VCE findings on teacher and community factors, and the Surgeon General's warning that isolation and lack of belonging are serious modern wounds.
teaching_candidate.falsifying_pressure: Weaken if teachers and practitioners report that no-distance teachings are safely practiced without visible support, if the distance field adds no predictive value to warning or repair coding, or if this teaching increases shame, dependence, or withdrawal in the target cohort.

practice_candidate.title: Name What Holds You
practice_candidate.practice_line: Before dropping effort, name the support you will keep.
practice_candidate.purpose: To test whether a no-distance teaching is being used as freedom from false striving or as an escape from needed support, conduct, and relationship.
practice_candidate.target_human_problem: loneliness, withdrawal, meaning loss, and spiritualized avoidance
practice_candidate.target_cohort: stable self-directed seekers who are drawn to teachings such as nothing to attain, already complete, no separate seeker, or practice as realization
practice_candidate.non_fit: Not for acute crisis, psychosis, mania, severe depression, dissociation, addiction withdrawal, unsafe relationships, or people already under trusted guidance who should ask their teacher, therapist, sponsor, clinician, or care network. Not for people whose main problem is under-motivation or avoidance of real duties.
practice_candidate.frequency: Once before making a practical decision based on a no-distance teaching, at most twice per week
practice_candidate.minimum_attempt: Use it three times across two weeks, only in ordinary stable conditions, before judging whether it helps.
steps: Write the teaching or sentence you are using, such as nothing to attain or there is no separate seeker.
Write the action you are tempted to take because of it: stop practicing, leave a group, ignore a duty, avoid repair, rest, ask for help, or continue as before.
Ask whether the teaching denies a real gap or denies false striving while keeping support in place.
Name the support you will keep for the next seven days: teacher, group, friend, schedule, vow, service, body care, therapy, recovery meeting, work duty, or honest conversation.
If you cannot name any support, do not make a large change today. Choose one ordinary human contact or duty instead.
After twenty-four hours, ask whether the teaching led to more care, clearer conduct, and less false striving, or to more isolation and avoidance.
notice: Whether the body relaxes because striving dropped, or tightens because support was removed.
Whether you become more available to people and duties, or more private and unreachable.
Whether the teaching reduces status-seeking, or quietly becomes a way to feel above ordinary help.
Whether a trusted person would recognize the action as more honest and more connected.
practice_candidate.caution: Stop if the exercise increases obsessive checking, shame, dissociation, withdrawal, contempt for ordinary life, or avoidance of needed care. This is not a substitute for clinical support, lineage guidance, recovery support, or urgent human help.
practice_candidate.weakens_if: Weakens if ordinary action planning works as well, if users feel more isolated or avoidant, or if the check becomes another performance of spiritual correctness.

tests.title: Distance Gate Prior-Art Check
tests.prediction: Near-neighbor scholarship will already cover non-instrumental practice, no-seeking, direct recognition, social scaffolding, doctrine as communal grammar, and meditation safety, but will not fully specify seeker-sought distance as a prior gate for deciding whether continuity-location analysis applies.
tests.result: Preliminary search found strong overlap with Dogen scholarship, Huangbo, Dzogchen sources, Shinran, Hartelius on nondual state language, VCE, extended religious cognition, Asad, Lindbeck, and recent internal records. Novelty should remain moderate and the idea should be treated as a narrowing heuristic, not doctrine.
tests.next_action: Search Japanese Zen scholarship on shusho-itto, Pure Land Other Power studies, Dzogchen pointing-out literature, and neo-Advaita critiques for an exact distance-before-support argument.

tests.title: No-Distance Support Coding
tests.prediction: If the model is useful, no-distance texts should place support in guards against false separation, such as posture, vow, teacher, devotion, conduct, or ordinary life, more often than in bridges to a later state. If support language looks the same as in gradual instrumental paths, the model weakens.
tests.next_action: Blind-code Dogen Bendowa and Genjokoan, Huangbo Transmission of Mind, Longchen Rabjam Trekcho, Shinran letters and Tannisho, and one gradual Theravada manual for distance status, support role, warning, and repair.

tests.title: Solo No-Distance Practice Reports
tests.prediction: Stable self-directed seekers using Name What Holds You should report less use of no-distance language for withdrawal, less abrupt dropping of support, and more concrete connection or duty within twenty-four hours. If isolation, shame, or avoidance rises, the practice and teaching weaken.
tests.next_action: Run a small opt-in diary pilot with clear exclusions for acute distress, dissociation, addiction withdrawal, severe depression, mania, unsafe relationships, and scrupulosity loops.

tests.title: Acceptance Without Passivity Analogy
tests.prediction: In psychotherapy, recovery, and burnout support, messages that reduce self-fighting should work best when paired with values, relationship, routine, or care, and should fail when heard as permission for passivity or isolation. If anti-striving messages work equally well without supports, the analogy weakens.
tests.next_action: Compare acceptance-based clinical protocols, recovery surrender language, and burnout rest guidance for whether support and action commitments prevent anti-effort language from becoming avoidance.
